prompt: Inside the Polymarket '2026 FIFA World Cup Winner' event, break down whale positions by country — show total whale value on each of the top 8 favorites (France, Spain, England, Brazil, Argentina, Germany, Portugal, Netherlands) so I can see where smart money is parked across the field. Include each country's current YES price and whale count alongside the dollar total.
expectedReason: prompt asks for an eight-item comparison
responsePreview:
As of May 2, 2026, the smart money for the 2026 FIFA World Cup is primarily concentrated on France and Spain. France leads all outcomes with over $1 million in total whale value, while the Netherlands sits at the bottom of the top eight favorites in terms of high-value backing.

The analysis was bounded to the top 8 requested favorites. The following breakdown is based on data retrieved from Polymarket:

| Country | YES Price | Total Whale Value | Whale Count |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| France | $0.1645 | $1,046,938.51 | 192 |
| Spain | $0.1525 | $965,423.50 | 162 |
| England | $0.1105 | $746,328.11 | 129 |
| Argentina | $0.0865 | $579,228.44 | 100 |
| Brazil | $0.0855 | $573,890.26 | 94 |
| Portugal | $0.0735 | $471,504.69 | 82 |
| Germany | $0.0515 | $337,159.83 | 63 |
| Netherlands | $0.0335 | $229,759.11 | 40 |

The associated bar chart illustrates a clear tiered structure in whale conviction: a "Big Three" consisting of France, Spain, and England holds significantly more smart money than the remaining field. While France has a slight edge in total capital, whale positions are relatively spread out, indicating no single consensus pick has emerged yet.
